name: planetary-boundaries
title: Planetary Boundaries
description: The nine planetary boundaries define the environmental limits within which humanity can safely operate. Based on Steffen et al. 2015 (Science) and subsequent updates. The dataset includes boundary definitions with safe-operating-space thresholds and temporal evolution data for each indicator from pre-industrial times to 2025. As of 2023, nine out of twelve tracked indicators have exceeded their boundary values.

sources.name: Steffen et al. 2015
sources.title: Planetary Boundaries: Guiding human development on a changing planet, Science 2015

sources.name: Richardson et al. 2023
sources.title: Earth beyond six of nine planetary boundaries, Science Advances 2023

sources.name: BastienGauthier/planetary-flag
sources.title: Planetary Flag — temporal evolution data compiled by Bastien Gauthier

resources.name: boundaries
resources.description: Definitions and current status of the nine planetary boundaries (12 sub-indicators). Each row describes one indicator: its safe operating space threshold, zone of uncertainty, and whether the boundary is currently exceeded based on the most recent available data.

fields.name: id
fields.description: Indicator ID as used in the Steffen 2015 paper (e.g. 1.0 = climate change, 5.1 = phosphorus cycle)

fields.name: name
fields.description: Short English name of the planetary boundary indicator

fields.name: unit
fields.description: Measurement unit for this indicator's value

fields.name: boundary_value
fields.description: The quantitative boundary value (safe operating space limit) as defined in Steffen et al. 2015

fields.name: boundary_zone_lower
fields.description: Lower bound of the zone of uncertainty (below this = clearly safe)

fields.name: boundary_zone_upper
fields.description: Upper bound of the zone of uncertainty (above this = high-risk zone)

fields.name: boundary_direction
fields.description: Whether higher values indicate greater pressure (positive) or lower values indicate greater pressure (negative)

fields.name: current_value
fields.description: Most recent observed value for this indicator

fields.name: current_year
fields.description: Year of the most recent observation

fields.name: boundary_exceeded
fields.description: Whether the current value has exceeded the boundary threshold

resources.name: boundary-evolution
resources.description: Temporal evolution of each planetary boundary indicator from pre-industrial times to ~2025. Data density varies by indicator: climate change (CO2) has monthly data since 1958, while others have sparse historical anchor points. Values are as observed/reported; no interpolation applied.

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year of observation. For climate change (CO2) this is a decimal date (e.g. 1958.2027 = February 1958). For all other indicators this is an integer year.

fields.name: boundary_id
fields.description: Indicator ID matching the boundaries table (e.g. 1.0, 5.1)

fields.name: boundary_name
fields.description: Full descriptive name of the boundary indicator including units context

fields.name: value
fields.description: Observed value of the indicator in the units specified in the boundaries table

views.name: boundary-status
views.title: Planetary Boundary Status (ratio to safe limit; excludes Novel Entities where safe limit = 0)

y.title: Current value as ratio of safe limit (1.0 = boundary)
